items.title: Quicklime With Less Than 98% Purity Falls Under CTH 2522: SC
items.summary:
The Supreme Court dismissed the Revenue’s appeals after observing that the classification dispute had already been settled in an earlier judgment upheld by the Court. The ruling confirms reliance on the precedent followed by CESTAT.
The post Quicklime With Less Than 98% Purity Falls Under CTH 2522: SC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Delhi Rejects Revenue Appeal Over Presumptive Taxation & Jewellery Additions
items.summary:
The ITAT Delhi held that once income higher than the presumptive rate under Section 44AD was declared, the assessee was not required to maintain detailed books or expense records. The addition based on estimated expenditure was deleted.
The post ITAT Delhi Rejects Revenue Appeal Over Presumptive Taxation & Jewellery Additions appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Remands Section 54F Dispute Due to Non-Consideration of Evidence
items.summary:
The ITAT Delhi held that the lower authorities failed to properly consider the assessee’s submissions and documentary evidence in a Section 54F dispute. The matter was remanded to the Assessing Officer for fresh adjudication after granting proper hearing opportunity.
The post ITAT Remands Section 54F Dispute Due to Non-Consideration of Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Rejects MAT Addition as Company Validly Opted for Section 115BAA Concessional taxation
items.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that MAT provisions under Section 115JB cannot apply once a company validly opts for concessional taxation under Section 115BAA. The Tribunal also relied on consistency as the department had accepted the option in earlier years.
The post ITAT Rejects MAT Addition as Company Validly Opted for Section 115BAA Concessional taxation appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Delhi Rejects Appeal as AO’s Situs Determines Territorial Jurisdiction
items.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that territorial jurisdiction depends on the location of the Assessing Officer handling the assessment. Since the assessee’s jurisdiction lay in Agra, the Delhi Bench dismissed the appeal.
The post ITAT Delhi Rejects Appeal as AO’s Situs Determines Territorial Jurisdiction appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Accepts Spam Folder Explanation as Reasonable Cause for Missing Tax Notice
items.summary:
The Tribunal ruled that the appellate authority should have adopted a liberal approach while considering additional evidence crucial to adjudication. The assessee was granted another opportunity to substantiate the exemption claim.
The post ITAT Accepts Spam Folder Explanation as Reasonable Cause for Missing Tax Notice appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Karnataka HC Quashes GST Order as Same Officer Handled Audit & Adjudication
items.summary:
The Karnataka High Court held that objections relating to an officer performing dual roles in audit and adjudication must be examined before merits are considered. The matter was remitted for fresh adjudication after jurisdictional findings.
The post Karnataka HC Quashes GST Order as Same Officer Handled Audit & Adjudication appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Quashes Section 153C Assessments As AO Recorded Improper Satisfaction
items.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that assessments under Section 153C were invalid as the Assessing Officer failed to record satisfaction in terms of the amended statutory requirement. The Tribunal quashed the assessments for lack of proper jurisdictional compliance.
The post ITAT Quashes Section 153C Assessments As AO Recorded Improper Satisfaction appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Deletes Section Addition for Demonetisation Cash Deposits Explained Through Cash Flow Statement
items.summary:
The ITAT Bangalore condoned a 28-day delay in filing appeal after accepting the assessee’s explanation regarding non-noticing of electronically communicated appellate order. The Tribunal adopted a justice-oriented approach.
The post ITAT Deletes Section Addition for Demonetisation Cash Deposits Explained Through Cash Flow Statement appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Reduces Demonetization Cash Deposit Addition to 10% Due to Supporting Evidence
items.summary:
The ITAT Rajkot reduced the addition on demonetization cash deposits after finding that the assessee had produced land records, cash flow statements, and other supporting evidence. The Tribunal restricted the addition to 10% of the disputed amount.
The post ITAT Reduces Demonetization Cash Deposit Addition to 10% Due to Supporting Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Withholding GST Refunds under Section 54(11): Need for Structured Framework
items.summary:
The article highlights concerns over indefinite withholding of GST refunds under Section 54(11) due to absence of statutory timelines. It proposes periodic review and reauthorization mechanisms to balance taxpayer rights and revenue protection.
The post Withholding GST Refunds under Section 54(11): Need for Structured Framework appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: GST Act Silence on Interest Cannot Defeat Refund Rights in Unconstitutional Levy Cases: AP HC
items.summary:
The Andhra Pradesh High Court held that refund arising from an unconstitutional GST levy carries a constitutional right to interest. The Court ruled that the State must compensate taxpayers for wrongful retention of money.
The post GST Act Silence on Interest Cannot Defeat Refund Rights in Unconstitutional Levy Cases: AP HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Common Issues raised in GST Department Audit
items.summary:
The article explains common GST audit objections relating to ITC, reverse charge liability, and turnover mismatches. It highlights that proper reconciliations, documentary evidence, and judicial precedents may help avoid demands and penalties.
The post Common Issues raised in GST Department Audit appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: State & Police To Blame For Tarikh Pe Tarikh, Not Just Judges: Allahabad HC
items.summary:
The Allahabad High Court observed that criminal case delays are caused not only by judicial officers but also by inadequate infrastructure, police non-cooperation, and forensic deficiencies. The Court issued multiple reform directions while rejecting a bail plea.
The post State & Police To Blame For Tarikh Pe Tarikh, Not Just Judges: Allahabad HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Delhi HC Orders GST Reimbursement Since Frozen Meat Supply Was Taxable
items.summary:
The Delhi High Court held that frozen chicken supplied to the Indian Army was not exempt from GST because it was supplied in unit containers. The Court directed reimbursement of GST paid by the supplier along with interest for delayed payment.
The post Delhi HC Orders GST Reimbursement Since Frozen Meat Supply Was Taxable appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: CBDT Notifies Form 10-IEA for Section 115BAC(6) Option; Filing Mandatory Before ITR Due Date
items.summary: CBDT has notified the new Form 10-IEA for taxpayers carrying on business who wish to opt out of the new default regime for AY 2026-27. The form must be filed on or before the due date u/s 139(1).

items.title: Supreme Court: Satisfaction Note Mandatory Before Section 153C Notice; Absence Renders Proceedings Void
items.summary: Three-judge bench holds that the satisfaction note is a jurisdictional pre-condition under Section 153C and must be recorded before issuance of notice, not after.

items.title: 55th GST Council: Prosecution Threshold Raised to Rs 3 Crore; Term Life Insurance Exempt from GST
items.summary: The 55th GST Council recommended raising the monetary threshold for prosecution under CGST Act from Rs 2 crore to Rs 3 crore. Term life insurance premiums proposed to be fully exempt.

items.title: ITAT Mumbai: Section 56(2)(x) Not Applicable in NCLT-Sanctioned Group Restructuring
items.summary: Special Bench holds that FMV deeming mechanism under Rule 11UA cannot override substance of a court-approved restructuring scheme. Addition of Rs 18.4 crore deleted.

items.title: CBIC Circular 231/2026: ITC Available on Canteen Services Under Factories Act Obligation
items.summary: CBIC ends long-standing uncertainty confirming GST Input Tax Credit is available on canteen services provided to factory workers under statutory obligation under Factories Act, 1948.
